title: IFU Workflow
text:
library(FITSio)
library(SpectralUnmix)

X <- readFITS("cube.fits")
Mat <- cube_to_matrix(X)
Mat[!is.finite(Mat)] <- 0
Mat <- pmax(Mat, 0)

title: IFU Workflow
text:
library(FITSio)
library(SpectralUnmix)

X <- readFITS("cube.fits")
Mat <- cube_to_matrix(X)
Mat[!is.finite(Mat)] <- 0
Mat <- pmax(Mat, 0)

title: IFU Workflow
text:
Fit and inspect

fit <- spectral_unmix(Mat, k = 5, lr = 0.01, niter = 5000)

plot(fit, type = "spectra")
plot(fit, type = "maps", nx = dim(X$imDat)[1], ny = dim(X$imDat)[2])
plot_reconstruction(fit, Mat, n = 6)

title: IFU Workflow
text:
Reconstruction outputs

maps <- predict(fit, type = "spatial")
cube_hat <- predict(
  fit,
  type = "cube",
  nx = dim(X$imDat)[1],
  ny = dim(X$imDat)[2]
)
resid <- residuals(
  fit,
  x = Mat,
  nx = dim(X$imDat)[1],
  ny = dim(X$imDat)[2]
)

title: IFU Workflow
text:
Metadata and redshift
When the input to cube_to_matrix() is a FITS-like list object, non-image entries are carried forward as metadata. That metadata can be recovered from the fitted object or reconstructed cube with cube_metadata().

fit <- spectral_unmix(Mat, k = 5, lr = 0.01, niter = 5000)
cube_metadata(fit)
cube_metadata(cube_hat)

If redshift information is stored in the FITS-side metadata, it will now be preserved as metadata, but it is not yet used automatically for rest-frame correction. For cubes with spatially varying velocity structure, rest-frame alignment or continuum preprocessing before factorization may be important.

title: SpectralUnmix
text: SpectralUnmix is an R package for smooth non-negative spectral unmixing of hyperspectral datasets and astronomical IFU cubes.

title: SpectralUnmix
text:
Model
The package solves
\[
X \approx A S
\]
where X is a spaxel-by-wavelength matrix, A contains spatial abundances, and S contains component spectra.

title: SpectralUnmix
text:
Workflow

flowchart LR
  A["IFU cube<br/>nx × ny × nλ"] --> B["cube_to_matrix()"]
  B --> C["spaxel × wavelength matrix X"]
  C --> D["spectral_unmix()"]
  D --> E["A: abundance maps"]
  D --> F["S: component spectra"]
  D --> G["AS: reconstruction"]
  E --> H["component_map()"]
  F --> I["plot(type = 'spectra')"]
  G --> J["predict(type = 'cube') / fitted()"]




flowchart LR
  A["IFU cube<br/>nx × ny × nλ"] --> B["cube_to_matrix()"]
  B --> C["spaxel × wavelength matrix X"]
  C --> D["spectral_unmix()"]
  D --> E["A: abundance maps"]
  D --> F["S: component spectra"]
  D --> G["AS: reconstruction"]
  E --> H["component_map()"]
  F --> I["plot(type = 'spectra')"]
  G --> J["predict(type = 'cube') / fitted()"]

title: SpectralUnmix
text:
Interpretation
Each spaxel spectrum is modeled as a non-negative combination of a small number of latent spectral components. The abundances vary across the field, while the component spectra are shared across all spaxels.

title: SpectralUnmix
text:
Main functions

spectral_unmix() fits the model.
cube_to_matrix() and matrix_to_cube() reshape IFU cubes.
basis() and coef() provide NMF-style accessors.
fitted(), predict(), and residuals() provide standard model methods.
plot() and plot_reconstruction() provide quick diagnostics.

title: SpectralUnmix
text:
Installation
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("RafaelSdeSouza/SpectralUnmix")
torch must be available in the local R installation.

title: Reference
text:
spectral_unmix()
summary()
print()

title: Getting Started
text: library(SpectralUnmix)

title: Getting Started
text:
Simulated example

demo <- simulate_ifu_cube(nx = 16, ny = 16, n_wave = 100)
fit <- spectral_unmix(
  demo$matrix,
  k = 3,
  lambda_smooth = 0.02,
  niter = 300,
  lr = 0.03
)

title: Getting Started
text:
Standard interface

print(fit)
summary(fit)
basis(fit)
coef(fit)

title: Getting Started
text:
Plots

plot(fit, type = "spectra", wavelength = demo$wavelength)
plot(fit, type = "maps", nx = demo$nx, ny = demo$ny)
plot(fit, type = "loss")
plot_reconstruction(fit, demo$matrix, n = 4, wavelength = demo$wavelength)

title: Getting Started
text:
What the outputs mean

fit$spatial contains one abundance weight per spaxel and per component.
fit$spectra contains the recovered component spectra.
fit$reconstruction is the model approximation A %*% S.
fitted(fit, nx, ny) and predict(fit, type = "cube", nx, ny) reshape that reconstruction back into a cube.
residuals(fit, x = ...) returns data minus reconstruction.

title: Getting Started
text:
Real library example

real_demo <- coelho_demo_spectra()
dim(real_demo$matrix)

[1]    4 2250

real_demo$metadata

           id                         file  teff logg feh
1  cool_giant t03000_g-0.5_p00p00_sed.fits  3000 -0.5 0.0
2 solar_dwarf t05750_g+4.5_p00p04_sed.fits  5750  4.5 0.0
3      a_star t09000_g+4.5_p00p00_sed.fits  9000  4.5 0.0
4    hot_star t18000_g+4.5_p02p00_sed.fits 18000  4.5 0.2



fit_real <- spectral_unmix(
  real_demo$matrix,
  k = 3,
  lambda_smooth = 0.001,
  niter = 400,
  lr = 0.03
)

plot(fit_real, type = "spectra", wavelength = real_demo$wavelength)
plot_reconstruction(
  fit_real,
  real_demo$matrix,
  n = 4,
  wavelength = real_demo$wavelength
)

title: Getting Started
text:
Common failure mode
If reconstructed spectra look almost identical across the cube and only the intensity changes, the fit has effectively collapsed toward a rank-1 solution or toward several nearly identical components. In practice this usually means one or more of the following:

the input cube is dominated by a single continuum shape
lambda_smooth is too large and forces spectra to become too similar
k is too small, or the optimization has converged to a poor local minimum
the data need preprocessing such as continuum normalization, masking, or rest-frame alignment before factorization

Useful checks are:

plot the recovered component spectra and see whether they are nearly identical
inspect the loss curve with plot(fit, type = "loss")
retry with multiple values of k
reduce lambda_smooth
shift spectra to the rest frame before fitting when redshift varies

title: Getting Started
text:
Stellar library compression

stellar_lib <- coelho_stellar_subset()
dim(stellar_lib$matrix)

[1]  100 2250

table(stellar_lib$metadata$type)


  a_f_star cool_dwarf cool_giant   hot_star solar_like 
        20         20         20         20         20 



fit_lib <- spectral_unmix(
  stellar_lib$matrix,
  k = 5,
  lambda_smooth = 0.001,
  niter = 500,
  lr = 0.03
)

plot(fit_lib, type = "spectra", wavelength = stellar_lib$wavelength)
plot_reconstruction(
  fit_lib,
  stellar_lib$matrix,
  n = 6,
  wavelength = stellar_lib$wavelength
)
